children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than the program effective date, and after notice and an opportunity for public comment in accordance with , the Authority shall establish a racetrack safety program applicable to all covered horses, covered persons, and covered horseraces in accordance with the registration of covered persons under .section 3053 of this titlesection 3054(d) of this title

children.heading: Considerations in development of safety program
children.label: 2 Considerations in development of safety program
children.text: In the development of the horseracing safety program for covered horses, covered persons, and covered horseraces, the Authority and the Commission shall take into consideration existing safety standards including the National Thoroughbred Racing Association Safety and Integrity Alliance Code of Standards, the International Federation of Horseracing Authority’s International Agreement on Breeding, Racing, and Wagering, and the British Horseracing Authority’s Equine Health and Welfare program.

children.heading: Elements of horseracing safety program
children.label: b Elements of horseracing safety program

children.text: A set of training and racing safety standards and protocols taking into account regional differences and the character of differing racing facilities.

A uniform set of training and racing safety standards and protocols consistent with the humane treatment of covered horses, which may include lists of permitted and prohibited practices or methods (such as crop use).

children.label: A
children.text: takes into account regional differences and the character of differing racing facilities; and

children.label: B
children.text: may include requirements for track surface design and consistency and established standard operating procedures related to track surface, monitoring, and maintenance (such as standardized seasonal assessment, daily tracking, and measurement).
A uniform set of track safety standards and protocols, that may include rules governing oversight and movement of covered horses and human and equine injury reporting and prevention.
Programs for injury and fatality data analysis, that may include pre- and post-training and race inspections, use of a veterinarian’s list, and concussion protocols.
The undertaking of investigations at racetrack and non-racetrack facilities related to safety violations.
Procedures for investigating, charging, and adjudicating violations and for the enforcement of civil sanctions for violations.
A schedule of civil sanctions for violations.
Disciplinary hearings, which may include binding arbitration, civil sanctions, and research.
Management of violation results.
Programs relating to safety and performance research and education.
An evaluation and accreditation program that ensures that racetracks in the United States meet the standards described in the elements of the Horseracing Safety Program.

children.heading: Standards for racetrack safety
children.label: 1 Standards for racetrack safety
children.text: The development, by the racetrack safety standing committee of the Authority in of uniform standards for racetrack and horseracing safety.section 3052(c)(2) of this title

children.heading: Standards for safety and performance accreditation
children.label: 2 Standards for safety and performance accreditation
i
children.text: safety and performance standards of accreditation for racetracks; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the process by which a racetrack may achieve and maintain accreditation by the Authority.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: The Authority may modify rules establishing the standards issued under subparagraph (A), as the Authority considers appropriate.

children.heading: Notice and comment
children.label: ii Notice and comment
children.text: The Commission shall publish in the Federal Register any proposed rule of the Authority, and provide an opportunity for public comment with respect to, any modification under clause (i) in accordance with .section 3053 of this title

children.heading: Extension of provisional or interim accreditation
children.label: C Extension of provisional or interim accreditation
children.text: The Authority may, by rule in accordance with , extend provisional or interim accreditation to a racetrack accredited by the National Thoroughbred Racing Association Safety and Integrity Alliance on a date before the program effective date.section 3053 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Not later than one year after the program effective date, and after notice and an opportunity for public comment in accordance with , the Authority, in consultation with the Commission, shall develop and maintain a nationwide database of racehorse safety, performance, health, and injury information for the purpose of conducting an epidemiological study.section 3053 of this title

children.heading: Collection of information
children.label: B Collection of information
children.text: In accordance with the registration of covered persons under , the Authority may require covered persons to collect and submit to the database described in subparagraph (A) such information as the Authority may require to further the goal of increased racehorse welfare.section 3054(d) of this title
